content_en:
were all pleased with the policies of the two righteous caliphs, Abu Bakr and Umar, in running the affairs of the Muslim nation. This being the case, *Umar did not command his successor to abide by the conduct of his two predecessors. Since following the policies of Abu Bakr and ‘Umar was not compulsory, it would be wrong to assume that * Ali was not made a caliph because he announced that he would act according to his best knowledge and capacity.

Lastly, if the question *Abdur-Rahmaan posed to both *Uthmaan and * Ali to which the two of them gave two different answers constituted the basic criterion in deciding who was to succeed *Umar, there was no point then in all the consultations and efforts that lasted for three whole nights in order to select the new caliph? So, if this issue was based on the conversation between the two candidates and *Abdur-Rahmaan, he could have asked them immediately after “Umar was buried and announced the name of the new caliph right away sparing himself and Muslims the trouble of consultations and deliberations.

Further, ~Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn *Awf could not have expected a different answer from that * Ali gave simply because Abu Bakr and *Umar ruled the Muslim state according to their best knowledge and capacity; i.e. they both relied on their personal opinion as to questions not tackled in the Glorious Quran and the Prophetic Tradition. For this reason, ~ Ali has

every right, it is incumbent on him, to lean on his personal judgment too, being an equal to both Abu Bakr and * Umar in status. Likewise, it would be unfair to accuse * Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn > Awf of rejecting personal opinion or preventing the caliph from making use of his knowledge that is based on the Glorious Quran and the Prophetic Tradition.

Therefore, the selection of the third righteous caliph was based on a wide-scale open referendum painstakingly conducted by Ibn ~Awf to ensure that the result would represent what the masses of Muslims wanted. Given such a detailed account, the issue of how ~Umar was selected as Muslims’ new caliph becomes crystal clear.

As soon as *Abdur-Rahmaan gave the pledge of allegiance to ~Uthmaan, people gathered to pledge loyalty to him while he was standing on the pulpit. That was on a Monday towards the end of Dhul-Hijjah, 23 year AH So he officially came to office at the start of the month of Muharam, 24 AH.

After he was given the pledge of allegiance, he went up the pulpit of Allah's Messenger, peace and blessings be upon him, and addressed the people saying: "Praise and Glory be to Allah, may He be exalted. Peace and blessings be upon the Prophet. We all live in a ephemeral world as passers-by. So make the best use of your lives according to your capacity for this world is full of deception. Let not this (worldly) present life deceive you, nor let the chief deceiver (Satan) let you astray.

"Learn a lesson from the lives of those before you. Then work hard and always remember Allah, for He forgets you not. Where are all the people who lived and populated this world and lead a luxurious life? Their lives came to an end. Discard

this world as Allah expects you to and seek the Hereafter which Allah describes saying: (A889 gSSly "SU By UY GH ee Go Sta Sl

"Set forth to them the similitude of the life of this world: it is like the rain which we send down from the skies: the earth's vegetation absorbs it, but soon it becomes dry stubble, which the winds do scatter: it is (only) Allah who prevails over all things. Wealth and sons are allurements of the life of this world: but the things that endure, good deeds, are best in the sight of thy Lord, as rewards, and best as (the foundation for) hopes." (Al-Kahf: 45-6)

It is obvious that the address ~ Uthmaan gave conveyed no agenda at all, as it made no reference to the policy he intended to follow in the future. Perhaps he thought he already did in the answer he gave ~ Abdur-Rahmaan while giving him the pledge of allegiance; that he would implement the Glorious Quran, the Prophetic tradition and the RightlyGuided principles of the two caliphs that preceded him. On the other hand, this address was rich in deep meanings as it portrayed the character of the new caliph, who turned seventy when he came to office, who refrained from all worldly pleasures seeking Allah's reward instead. One is not to be surprised that his first address to his subjects would be to admonish and guide them righteously so that they would follow his example of piety and asceticism.

{t was not too long before the new caliph publicly announced his policies in running the Muslim state. They were expressed in the messages and announcements he sent to the governors in the various provinces of the state. In his very first read, he admonished his governors to spare no effort in serving the people and never to exploit them. He advised them to take care of Muslims’ affairs, give them their due rights and have them fulfill their obligations.

The second message was sent to the commanders of armies on borderlines. ~ Uthmaan reminded them that they were the defenders of Muslims. He asked them to follow the clear guidelines ~ Umar had laid down for them with no alterations.

He next wrote to the Kharaaj collectors wherein he reminded them that Allah accepts nothing but what is right. They therefore ought to take none but what is their due and let people have what is rightfully theirs. He urged them to treasure what they are entrusted with and warned them not be the first to betray those who placed their trust in them. He further warned them not to oppress the orphans and those under their protection, for Allah will revenge on those who oppress them.

As regards his message to the public, *Uthmaan called on his subjects to closely abide by the rulings of Islam. He warned them not to not let worldly goals distract them from what is in their best interest. He warned them that this nation would turn from consensus and truths to innovation of ideas. He pointed that the Muslim nation would witness three things in the time to come: people will amass wealth, have children begotten by their slaves, and Arabs and non-Arab will read the Quran, for the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, said: "Ignorance is the cause of disbelief." for when people are ignorant of something, they will resort to innovations.

The First Case before *Uthmaan:

According to historians, * Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn Abu Bakr said: ~Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn *Auf saw the dagger with which “Umar was killed with Al-Hurmudhaan and Jufaynah. He also said when ~Umar was stabbed: "I passed by Abu Lu'lu'ah, “Umar's murderer, while he was secretly talking to AlHurmudhaan and Jufaynah, one of the Christians of Al-Hayrah. When | caught them off guard, they were startled and dropped a two-edged dagger with its handle in the middle. So see whether it is the one."

No sooner had people pledged allegiance to ~ Uthmaan as the new caliph than *Ubaydelalah Ibn ~Umar was brought before him in the mosque in order to pass a judgment in his case. “Uthmaan sought the opinion of the elderly among the Makkan Emigrants and the Medinan Supporters saying: "I need your advice as regards this person who caused a rupture in Islam with his crime." * Ali said: "He should be killed." Some Makkan Emigrants pointed: "* Umar was killed yesterday and his son will be killed today!"

Hence, *Uthmaan was confronted with difficult, not to mention delicate situation in his early days in office. But he was able to handle the situation immediately. The caliph said: "1 am the guardian of those killed, so I will take blood money instead, and it will be deducted from my money." No doubt that the caliph displayed obvious wisdom in dealing with a very thorny and complicated issue.

The reason why that was such a precarious situation was because ~Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, a prominent figure in the Islamic world, was killed the day before, and the perpetrator was put to death right away. Killing Abu Lu'lu'ah was indeed the just penalty, according to the law of equality in punishment (Al-Qisaas), since it was one soul that executed the crime.

*Ubaydelalah Ibn ~Umar, having heard the story of *Abdur-Rabmaan Ibn Abu Bakr, had a different opinion. He believed that all three of them: Abu Lu'lu'ah, AlHurmudhaan and Jufaynah collaborated and teamed up to assassinate his beloved father. But Abu Lu'lu'ah was the one chosen to put the plan in effect. Still, the crime was masterminded by the three men. As a result, ~Ubaydelalah grew furious as he contemplated the idea that his father, ‘Umar [bn Al-Khattab, was no ordinary man. He was the caliph of the Muslim state. Killing him would not be a mere crime to be committed by an ordinary criminal. There must be several motives and collaborators behind it. That was especially true since the three criminals were nursed bitter hatred and harbored vindictive feelings against Islam and Muslims. That went double for their caliph who conquered their countries and subjected them and their nations to the rule of Muslims. It was only natural therefore for them to commit such a hideous crime.

For three days while his father suffered the pain of the injury, his son was fuming with rage against the devilish 289

perpetrators. Right upon his father's death, * Ubaydelalah did not think twice to avenge his father's death. However, he crossed the line by he killing a girl who had no hand in killing the caliph. No Abu Lu'lu'ah's daughter was undoubtedly the victim of * Ubaidullah's rash action,
content_id:
semuanya ridha dengan kebijakan dua khalifah yang saleh, Abu Bakr dan Umar, dalam mengelola urusan umat Islam. Dengan demikian, Umar tidak memerintahkan penggantinya untuk mengikuti perilaku kedua pendahulunya. Karena mengikuti kebijakan Abu Bakr dan Umar tidak bersifat wajib, maka keliru jika beranggapan bahwa Ali tidak diangkat menjadi khalifah karena ia menyatakan akan bertindak sesuai dengan ilmu dan kemampuannya yang terbaik.

Terakhir, jika pertanyaan yang diajukan Abdurrahman kepada Utsman dan Ali—yang keduanya memberikan jawaban berbeda—merupakan kriteria utama dalam menentukan siapa yang akan menggantikan Umar, maka tidak ada gunanya seluruh konsultasi dan upaya yang berlangsung selama tiga malam penuh untuk memilih khalifah baru. Jadi, jika masalah ini didasarkan pada percakapan antara kedua kandidat dan Abdurrahman, ia bisa saja langsung bertanya kepada mereka segera setelah Umar dimakamkan dan mengumumkan nama khalifah baru seketika itu juga, tanpa perlu merepotkan dirinya dan umat Islam dengan konsultasi dan musyawarah panjang.

Lebih jauh lagi, Abdurrahman Ibn Awf tidak mungkin mengharapkan jawaban yang berbeda dari yang diberikan Ali, semata-mata karena Abu Bakr dan Umar memerintah negara Islam sesuai dengan ilmu dan kemampuan terbaik mereka; yakni, keduanya mengandalkan pendapat pribadi mereka dalam persoalan-persoalan yang tidak diatur dalam Al-Quran yang Mulia dan Sunnah Nabawiyyah. Oleh karena itu, Ali

memiliki setiap hak, bahkan kewajiban, untuk bersandar pada penilaian pribadinya pula, karena ia setara dengan Abu Bakr dan Umar dalam kedudukan. Demikian pula, tidaklah adil untuk menuduh Abdurrahman Ibn Awf menolak pendapat pribadi atau melarang khalifah memanfaatkan ilmunya yang bersumber dari Al-Quran yang Mulia dan Sunnah Nabawiyyah.

Oleh karena itu, pemilihan khalifah ketiga yang saleh didasarkan pada referendum terbuka berskala luas yang dilaksanakan dengan susah payah oleh Ibnu Awf untuk memastikan hasilnya benar-benar mencerminkan kehendak mayoritas umat Islam. Dengan penjelasan yang terperinci ini, persoalan bagaimana Usman dipilih sebagai khalifah baru umat Islam menjadi sangat jelas.

Begitu Abdurrahman memberikan baiat kepada Utsman, orang-orang pun berkumpul untuk menyatakan kesetiaan kepadanya sementara ia berdiri di atas mimbar. Itu terjadi pada hari Senin menjelang akhir Dzulhijjah, tahun 23 H. Maka ia resmi menjabat pada awal bulan Muharram, 24 H.

Setelah menerima baiat, ia naik ke mimbar Rasulullah shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam dan berpidato kepada khalayak seraya berkata: "Segala puji dan keagungan milik Allah, semoga Dia dimuliakan. Shalawat dan salam atas Nabi. Kita semua hidup di dunia yang fana ini sebagai musafir. Maka manfaatkanlah hidupmu sebaik-baiknya sesuai kemampuanmu, karena dunia ini penuh dengan tipu daya. Janganlah kehidupan dunia yang sekarang ini menipu kalian, dan jangan pula penipu ulung (setan) menyesatkan kalian.

"Ambillah pelajaran dari kehidupan orang-orang sebelum kalian. Kemudian bekerja keraslah dan senantiasa ingatlah kepada Allah, karena Dia tidak melupakanmu. Di mana semua orang yang pernah hidup, memenuhi dunia ini, dan menjalani kehidupan yang mewah? Kehidupan mereka telah berakhir. Tinggalkanlah

dunia ini sebagaimana Allah mengharapkanmu, dan carilah Akhirat yang Allah gambarkan dengan firman-Nya: (A889 gSSly "SU By UY GH ee Go Sta Sl

"Dan berikanlah kepada mereka sebuah perumpamaan tentang kehidupan dunia ini: ia seperti air hujan yang Kami turunkan dari langit, lalu tumbuh-tumbuhan bumi menyerapnya, kemudian menjadi kering dan diterbangkan oleh angin. Dan Allah Mahakuasa atas segala sesuatu. Harta dan anak-anak adalah perhiasan kehidupan dunia; tetapi amal-amal saleh yang kekal, lebih baik pahalanya di sisi Tuhanmu serta lebih baik untuk menjadi harapan." (Al-Kahfi: 45-46)

Sudah jelas bahwa pidato yang disampaikan Utsman sama sekali tidak mengandung agenda apapun, karena tidak menyinggung kebijakan yang hendak ia jalankan di masa mendatang. Barangkali ia mengira telah menyampaikannya melalui jawaban yang ia berikan kepada Abdurrahman saat memberikan baiat; bahwa ia akan melaksanakan Al-Quran yang Mulia, Sunnah Nabawiyyah, dan prinsip-prinsip khalifah rasyidin yang mendahuluinya. Di sisi lain, pidato ini kaya akan makna yang mendalam karena menggambarkan karakter khalifah baru tersebut, yang telah berusia tujuh puluh tahun ketika menjabat, yang menjauhi segala kesenangan duniawi demi mengharapkan ridha Allah. Tidak mengherankan bahwa pidato pertamanya kepada rakyatnya adalah untuk menasehati dan membimbing mereka dengan benar agar mereka meneladani sikap taqwa dan zuhudnya.

Tidak lama kemudian, khalifah baru secara terbuka mengumumkan kebijakannya dalam mengelola negara Islam. Kebijakan-kebijakan tersebut tercermin dalam pesan-pesan dan pengumuman yang ia kirimkan kepada para gubernur di berbagai provinsi negara. Dalam surat pertamanya, ia menasehati para gubernurnya agar tidak kenal lelah dalam melayani rakyat dan tidak mengeksploitasi mereka. Ia mengarahkan mereka untuk memperhatikan urusan umat Islam, memberikan hak-hak mereka, dan mendorong mereka menunaikan kewajiban-kewajibannya.

Surat kedua dikirimkan kepada para panglima angkatan bersenjata di garis perbatasan. Utsman mengingatkan mereka bahwa merekalah para pembela umat Islam. Ia meminta mereka untuk mengikuti panduan yang jelas yang telah ditetapkan Umar bagi mereka tanpa ada perubahan.

Selanjutnya ia menulis kepada para pemungut kharaj, mengingatkan mereka bahwa Allah tidak menerima kecuali yang benar. Oleh karena itu, mereka hanya boleh mengambil apa yang menjadi hak mereka dan membiarkan rakyat mendapatkan apa yang menjadi hak mereka. Ia mendorong mereka untuk menjaga apa yang dipercayakan kepada mereka dan memperingatkan mereka agar tidak menjadi orang pertama yang mengkhianati mereka yang telah memberikan kepercayaan. Ia juga memperingatkan mereka agar tidak menindas anak-anak yatim dan orang-orang yang berada di bawah perlindungan mereka, karena Allah akan membalas mereka yang menindas golongan ini.

Adapun pesannya kepada masyarakat umum, Utsman menyeru rakyatnya untuk senantiasa berpegang teguh pada hukum-hukum Islam. Ia memperingatkan mereka agar tidak membiarkan tujuan-tujuan duniawi memalingkan mereka dari apa yang terbaik bagi kepentingan mereka. Ia memperingatkan bahwa umat ini akan berpaling dari ijma' dan kebenaran menuju inovasi pemikiran. Ia menunjukkan bahwa umat Islam akan menyaksikan tiga hal di masa mendatang: orang-orang akan menumpuk harta, memiliki anak-anak dari budak perempuan mereka, dan orang Arab maupun non-Arab akan membaca Al-Quran, karena Nabi shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam bersabda: "Kebodohan adalah penyebab kekufuran." Sebab ketika orang-orang bodoh tentang sesuatu, mereka akan beralih kepada bid'ah.

Kasus Pertama di Hadapan Utsman:

Menurut para sejarawan, Abdurrahman Ibn Abu Bakr berkata: Abdurrahman Ibn Awf melihat belati yang digunakan untuk membunuh Umar ada pada Al-Hurmuzan dan Jufainah. Ia juga berkata ketika Umar ditikam: "Aku melewati Abu Lu'lu'ah, pembunuh Umar, saat ia sedang berbicara secara rahasia dengan Al-Hurmuzan dan Jufainah, salah seorang Kristen dari Al-Hirah. Ketika aku mengejutkan mereka, mereka terkejut dan menjatuhkan belati bermata dua dengan pegangannya di bagian tengah. Maka lihatlah apakah itu belatinya."

Tidak lama setelah orang-orang membaiat Utsman sebagai khalifah baru, Ubaidullah Ibn Umar dibawa ke hadapannya di masjid untuk diputuskan perkaranya. Utsman meminta pendapat para sesepuh dari kalangan Muhajirin Makkah dan Anshar Madinah seraya berkata: "Aku membutuhkan saran kalian mengenai orang ini yang telah menyebabkan perpecahan dalam Islam dengan kejahatannya." Ali berkata: "Ia harus dibunuh." Sebagian Muhajirin Makkah mengatakan: "Umar dibunuh kemarin dan anaknya akan dibunuh hari ini!"

Maka, Utsman dihadapkan pada situasi yang sulit, belum lagi rumit, di awal masa jabatannya. Namun ia mampu menangani situasi tersebut dengan segera. Sang khalifah berkata: "Aku adalah wali dari mereka yang terbunuh, maka aku akan mengambil diyat sebagai gantinya, dan akan dipotong dari hartaku." Tidak diragukan lagi bahwa sang khalifah menunjukkan kebijaksanaan yang nyata dalam menangani persoalan yang sangat pelik dan rumit.

Alasan mengapa situasi itu begitu berbahaya adalah karena Umar Ibn Al-Khattab, tokoh terkemuka dalam dunia Islam, telah dibunuh sehari sebelumnya, dan pelakunya langsung dihukum mati. Membunuh Abu Lu'lu'ah memang merupakan hukuman yang adil, sesuai dengan hukum qishash, karena hanya satu jiwa yang melaksanakan kejahatan tersebut.

Ubaidullah Ibn Umar, setelah mendengar kisah dari Abdurrahman Ibn Abu Bakr, memiliki pendapat yang berbeda. Ia yakin bahwa ketiganya: Abu Lu'lu'ah, Al-Hurmuzan, dan Jufainah bersekongkol dan bekerja sama untuk membunuh ayahnya yang ia cintai. Namun Abu Lu'lu'ah adalah orang yang dipilih untuk melaksanakan rencana tersebut. Tetap saja, kejahatan itu didalangi oleh ketiga orang tersebut. Akibatnya, Ubaidullah menjadi sangat marah ketika ia merenungkan kenyataan bahwa ayahnya, Umar Ibn Al-Khattab, bukanlah orang biasa. Ia adalah khalifah negara Islam. Membunuhnya bukanlah sekadar kejahatan biasa yang dilakukan oleh penjahat biasa. Pasti ada beberapa motif dan kolaborator di baliknya. Hal itu semakin jelas mengingat ketiga penjahat tersebut memendam kebencian mendalam dan dendam kesumat terhadap Islam dan umat Islam. Terlebih lagi terhadap khalifah mereka yang telah menaklukkan negeri-negeri mereka dan menundukkan mereka beserta bangsa-bangsa mereka di bawah kekuasaan umat Islam. Maka sudah wajar jika mereka melakukan kejahatan keji semacam itu.

Selama tiga hari saat ayahnya menanggung kesakitan akibat luka tersebut, putranya membara dengan amarah terhadap para pelaku keji itu.

Tepat setelah kematian ayahnya, 'Ubaidullah tidak berpikir panjang untuk membalas kematian sang ayah. Namun, ia melampaui batas dengan membunuh seorang gadis yang tidak memiliki andil dalam pembunuhan sang khalifah. Tidak diragukan lagi, putri Abu Lu'lu'ah adalah korban dari tindakan gegabah 'Ubaidullah.
content_ar: were all pleased with the policies of the two الصالح caliphs, أبو بكر and عمر, in running the affairs of the مسلم nation. This being the case, *عمر did not command his successor to abide by the السلوك of his two predecessors. بما أن following the policies of أبو بكر and ‘عمر was not compulsory, it would be الخطأ to assume that * علي was not made a caliph لأن he announced that he would act وفقاً لـ his best العلم and capacity. وأخيراً, إذا the question *Abdur-Rahmaan posed to both *Uthmaan and * علي to which the two of them gave two different answers constituted the الأساسي criterion in deciding who was to succeed *عمر, there was no point then in all the consultations and efforts that lasted for three whole nights من أجل select the new caliph? لذا, إذا this issue was based on the conversation between the two candidates and *Abdur-Rahmaan, he could have asked them immediately بعد “عمر was buried and announced the name of the new caliph الحق away sparing himself and المسلمون the trouble of consultations and deliberations. Further, ~Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn *Awf could not have expected a different answer from that * علي gave simply لأن أبو بكر and *عمر ruled the مسلم state وفقاً لـ their best العلم and capacity; i.e. they both relied on their personal opinion as to questions not tackled in the Glorious القرآن الكريم and the Prophetic التراث. For this العقل, ~ علي has every الحق, يجب on him, to lean on his personal judgment أيضاً, being an equal to both أبو بكر and * عمر in status. Likewise, it would be unfair to accuse * Abdur-Rahmaan Ibn > Awf of rejecting personal opinion or preventing the caliph from making use of his العلم that is based on the Glorious القرآن الكريم and the Prophetic التراث. لذلك, the selection of the ثالثاً الصالح caliph was based on a wide-scale open referendum painstakingly conducted by Ibn ~Awf لضمان that the result would represent what the masses of المسلمون wanted. Given such a detailed account, the issue of how ~عمر was selected as المسلمون’ new caliph becomes crystal clear. As soon as *Abdur-Rahmaan gave the pledge of allegiance to ~Uthmaan, people gathered to pledge loyalty to him بينما he was standing on the pulpit. That was on a Monday towards the end of Dhul-Hijjah, 23 year AH لذا he officially came to office at the start of the month of Muharam, 24 AH. بعد he was given the pledge of allegiance, he went up the pulpit of الله's Messenger, peace and البركات be upon him, and addressed the people saying: "Praise and Glory be to الله, may He be exalted. Peace and البركات be upon النبي ﷺ. We all live in a ephemeral الدنيا as passers-by. لذا make the best use of your lives وفقاً لـ your capacity for this الدنيا is full of deception. Let not this (worldly) present الحياة deceive you, nor let the chief deceiver (الشيطان) let you astray. "Learn a lesson from the lives of those قبل you. Then work hard and always
